title: Export endpoint is authenticated but not authorization-gated
reasoning: The route exports the full address-label snapshot plus all forensic reports after checking only that some session exists. The inline comment describes this as a maintainer-driven export/reimport path, and the response contains bulk operational data; if normal signed-in dashboard users exist, they can download the entire dataset without an explicit maintainer/admin/domain authorization check at this route boundary.
recommendation: Add an explicit authorization check for the role/domain/group that is allowed to manage address labels and forensic reports before reading Redis/blob data, and return 403 for authenticated but unauthorized users.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests cover unauthenticated 401 and successful export for a mocked session, but they do not model an authenticated user who lacks export privileges or assert that a maintainer/admin predicate is enforced.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock getAuthSession to return an authenticated non-maintainer user and assert GET returns 403 without calling getLabels or getAllReports; add a separate test for an authorized maintainer session that preserves the current 200 behavior.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/app/api/address-labels/export/route.ts plus the route test file to cover authorized and unauthorized authenticated sessions.

history.note: Fixed by requiring an authenticated maintainer-domain session before exporting labels/reports; added unauthorized and authorized export route coverage.
